mapping.a_element: discrete age-of-infection structure (a survival/lifetime distribution over how long an individual has been infected)
mapping.b_element: per-round URL survival rate alpha and URL-lifetime structure (how long a URL persists across crawl rounds)

mapping.a_element: population of individuals in epidemiological compartments
mapping.b_element: evolving population of URLs sampled by the crawl

brief_justification: Paper A is a nonlinear contagion/transmission model: infected individuals exert a force of infection on susceptibles via exposure, with viral-load- and infection-age-dependent transmission, so items create new infected items (a branching/renewal contagion process). Paper B is a linear urn sampling model: each URL independently survives (alpha) or is replaced and is discovered by sampling (coverage c), with no item causing another to change state; its (alpha, c) parameters are estimated by reconciling two projections (containment and discovery curve). The only overlap is the generic notion of a time-indexed changing population with survival rates; the governing machinery (interacting transmission vs. independent sampling/turnover) differs, so they do not share an underlying method.
